rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner'S Amended Petition For Order Directing Transfer Of Possession Of Personal Property Belonging To Estate (Other)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Martha Wash's Demurrer to Amended Petition for Order Directing Transfer of Possession of Personal Property Belonging to the Estate. Movant's request for judicial notice of various pleadings and documents filed in this matter is GRANTED, pursuant to Evid. Code secs. 452(d) and 453. The Opposition's request for judicial notice of an order and documents filed in this matter is GRANTED, pursuant to Evid. Code secs. 452(d) and 453. A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading, based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318; County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 996, 1008, 1009.) The court notes, judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in those materials. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) "A court may take judicial notice of the existence of each document in a court file, but can only take judicial notice of the truth of facts asserted in documents such as orders, findings of fact and conclusions of law, and judgments. [citation.] [ ] A mistaken notion exists that taking judicial notice of court records means noticing " '... the existence of facts asserted in [all the documents in] a court file ... a court cannot take judicial notice of hearsay allegations as being true, just because they are part of a court record or file....' " (Garcia v. Sterling (1985) 176 Cal. App. 3d 17, 21-22, quoting Day v. Sharp (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 904, 914, emphasis in original.) A tentative ruling is preliminary and non-binding by definition (California Rules of Court 3.1308). Unless adopted as an order of the court after hearing, it is neither a final order nor a finding of the court. Per the Notice of Demurrer, Movant demurs to the Amended Petition under "California Code of Civil Procedure [sec.] 430.10(d), 430.10(e), and 430.10(f) as the Amended Petition contains a defect of parties, does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action, and it is uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible. Petitioner's verified admissions bar recovery under the causes of action pled." (Notice, 2:6-9.) As discussed in the Memorandum of Points and Authorities, Movant demurs generally to the pleadings on the grounds that the Amended Petition (1) fails to plead so as to join Movant as a party in her capacity as trustee; (2) facially requests an order on will reformation but does not plead the elements of a cause of action for reformation; (3) pleads mutually exclusive legal theories; (4) uncertainty in the pleadings such that Movant cannot tell what she is supposed to respond to; (5) failure to state sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action, as to each cause of action, plus the request for reformation Movant has understood to exist the face of the Amended Petition but which is not pleaded as a cause of action.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner'S Amended Petition For Order Directing Transfer Of Possession Of Personal Property Belonging To Estate (Other) (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication, cannot consider the party's ability to prove their factual allegations (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.; Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) Cal.4th 992, 998, Gray v. Dignity Health (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 225.) Demurrers test legal sufficiency based on the allegations as pleaded, without regard to the plaintiff's ability to prove the alleged facts. (see, Gray v. Dignity Health (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 225.) The sole question raised by demurrer is whether the pleading is facially-not factually-deficient. Demurrers challenge only the legal sufficiency of pleadings on their face, not the truth or adequacy of factual allegations or evidence (Code of Civil Proc. sec. 430.30(a)). A ruling on a demurrer does not determine contested factual issues relating to the merits. Demurrer under section 430.10(d) regarding joinder of Movant in whatever capacity or capacities Petitioner is alleging is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Demurrer under section 430.10(e) to the First Cause of Action under Probate Code sec. 850(a)(2)(D) is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Demurrer under section 430.10(e) to the Second Cause of Action under Probate Code sec. 13111(a) is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Demurrer under section 430.10(e) to the Third Cause of Action under Probate Code sec. 13111(b) is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND to plead this cause of action consistent with the language of the statute it is facially brought under, Probate Code sec. 13111(b). Demurrer under section 430.10(e) to the Fourth Cause of Action under Probate Code sec. 859 is OVERRULED, insofar as the argument for demurrer to this cause of action on these grounds is that Petitioner's legal assertions are incorrect, determination of which is not within the scope of a demurrer. The general demurrer under section 430.10(f) is OVERRULED. Under section 430.10(f), "The pleading is uncertain. As used in this subdivision, 'uncertain'; includes ambiguous and unintelligible." Per the Notice of Demurrer, Movant argues the Amended Petition is "uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible." Uncertainty is the broad ground for demurrer that covers any defective statement making the cause of action or any part of it unclear. Ambiguity is narrower, available when the complaint is doubtful in meaning. A complaint is unintelligible when its meaning cannot be comprehended at all. (see, e.g., Craig v. Los Angeles (1941) 44 C.A.2d 71, 73.) The Amended Petition, although subject to demurrer sustained with leave amend to plead specific additional facts to complete the elements of specific causes of action under section 430.10(e), is sufficiently pleaded to apprise Movant of the allegations being made against her and the relief sought, as evidenced by Movant's arguments in the Demurrer regarding Petitioner's ability to prove her allegations, and thus is not subject to demurrer under section 430.10(f). (See, Butler v. Sequeira (1950) 100 C.A.2d 143, 145-146.) Demurrer on the basis that Movant perceives a cause of action for reformation that is otherwise not pleaded on the face of the Amended Petition as a separate cause of action is OVERRULED. Petitioner shall file a further amended petition within ten (10) days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. Movant shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing.
